content: Just as curiosity-driven intrinsic motivation (ICM/RND) shapes how autonomous agents prioritize exploring uncertain new environments to build useful memory, active learning agent memory systems can be redesigned to 'forget' low-curiosity data by default, retaining only information that triggers intrinsic novelty signals for future task adaptation. This creates a self-sustaining cycle where an agent's curiosity prunes its memory, and the pruned memory in turn focuses its curiosity on high-impact unlearned knowledge.
